4. The cases `k = n`, `k = m`, `n = m`, and the off-support zero cases are all
   componentwise `simp` cases of the same statement; do not split them into
   four separate theorem surfaces.

The exact helper theorem for the OS Hilbert vectors is the corresponding class
linearity in the OS quotient completion:

```lean
private theorem osHilbertClass_linear_comb_single_single
    (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
    {n m : ℕ}
    (f : euclideanPositiveTimeSubmodule (d := d) n)
    (g : euclideanPositiveTimeSubmodule (d := d) m)
    (a b : ℂ) :
    let F₀ : PositiveTimeBorchersSequence d :=
      PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.single n f.1 f.2
    let G₀ : PositiveTimeBorchersSequence d :=
      PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.single m g.1 g.2
    (((show OSPreHilbertSpace OS from (⟦a • F₀ + b • G₀⟧)) :
        OSHilbertSpace OS)) =
      a • (((show OSPreHilbertSpace OS from (⟦F₀⟧)) :
        OSHilbertSpace OS)) +
      b • (((show OSPreHilbertSpace OS from (⟦G₀⟧)) :
        OSHilbertSpace OS))
```

Proof transcript:

1. `dsimp` the local `F₀` and `G₀`.
2. Change the left OS pre-Hilbert class to
   `a • (⟦F₀⟧ : OSPreHilbertSpace OS) + b • (⟦G₀⟧ : OSPreHilbertSpace OS)`.
   This is justified by the quotient `Add`/`SMul` instances in
   `SchwingerOS.lean`.
3. Push the equality into the completion with
   `UniformSpace.Completion.coe_add` and
   `UniformSpace.Completion.coe_smul`.
4. Finish by `simpa`.

The four OS-side expanded-boundary bookkeeping instantiations are exactly:

```lean
section43Transport_component_linear_comb_single_single
  (d := d) φ ψ f g hφf hψg 1 1
section43Transport_component_linear_comb_single_single
  (d := d) φ ψ f g hφf hψg 1 (-1)
section43Transport_component_linear_comb_single_single
  (d := d) φ ψ f g hφf hψg 1 Complex.I
section43Transport_component_linear_comb_single_single
  (d := d) φ ψ f g hφf hψg 1 (-Complex.I)

osHilbertClass_linear_comb_single_single
  (d := d) OS f g 1 1
osHilbertClass_linear_comb_single_single
  (d := d) OS f g 1 (-1)
osHilbertClass_linear_comb_single_single
  (d := d) OS f g 1 Complex.I
osHilbertClass_linear_comb_single_single
  (d := d) OS f g 1 (-Complex.I)
```

Use `simpa [sub_eq_add_neg, one_smul]` to match the theorem outputs to the
four terms `xF + xG`, `xF - xG`, `xF + Complex.I • xG`, and
`xF - Complex.I • xG` produced by
`selfAdjointSpectralBoundaryValueOffdiagCLM_apply`.

With that transformed-surface `hPsi` theorem, the canonical witness is
identified with the OS holomorphic matrix element by direct application of the
existing reducer:

```lean
private theorem
    bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShiftCanonicalExtension_imag_eq_osHolomorphicValue_of_fourierLaplaceTransform
    (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d) (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
    {n m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
    (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n) (ψ : SchwartzNPoint d m)
    (hψ_compact : HasCompactSupport (ψ : NPointDomain d m → ℂ))
    (f : euclideanPositiveTimeSubmodule (d := d) n)
    (g : euclideanPositiveTimeSubmodule (d := d) m)
    (hf_compact : HasCompactSupport (f.1 : NPointDomain d n → ℂ))
    (hg_compact : HasCompactSupport (g.1 : NPointDomain d m → ℂ))
    (hφ_transform :
      section43FrequencyProjection (d := d) n φ =
        section43FourierLaplaceTransformComponent d n f)
    (hψ_transform :
      section43FrequencyProjection (d := d) m ψ =
        section43FourierLaplaceTransformComponent d m g) :
    ∀ t : ℝ, 0 < t →
      bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShiftCanonicalExtension
        (d := d) OS lgc φ ψ hψ_compact ((t : ℂ) * Complex.I) =
      OSInnerProductTimeShiftHolomorphicValue (d := d) OS lgc
        (PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.single n f.1 f.2)
        (PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.single m g.1 g.2) (t : ℂ)
```

Proof transcript:

1. Apply
   `bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShiftCanonicalExtension_imag_eq_osHolomorphicValue_of_ambient_descended_psiZ_boundaryValue_eq`
   with `f := f.1`, `hf_ord := f.2`, `g := g.1`, `hg_ord := g.2`.
2. Supply its `hPsi` hypothesis with
   `descendedPsiZ_boundaryValue_eq_osSpectral_of_fourierLaplaceTransform`.

The direct Lemma-4.2 adapter for transformed representatives becomes formal
after the two non-finite-height packets above are proved:

```lean
private theorem lemma42_matrix_element_time_interchange_of_fourierLaplaceTransform
    (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d) (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
    {n m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
    (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n) (ψ : SchwartzNPoint d m)
    (hψ_compact : HasCompactSupport (ψ : NPointDomain d m → ℂ))
    (f : euclideanPositiveTimeSubmodule (d := d) n)
    (g : euclideanPositiveTimeSubmodule (d := d) m)
    (hf_compact : HasCompactSupport (f.1 : NPointDomain d n → ℂ))
    (hg_compact : HasCompactSupport (g.1 : NPointDomain d m → ℂ))
    (hφ_transform :
      section43FrequencyProjection (d := d) n φ =
        section43FourierLaplaceTransformComponent d n f)
    (hψ_transform :
      section43FrequencyProjection (d := d) m ψ =
        section43FourierLaplaceTransformComponent d m g) :
    bvt_W OS lgc (n + m) (φ.conjTensorProduct ψ) =
      OS.S (n + m) (ZeroDiagonalSchwartz.ofClassical (f.1.osConjTensorProduct g.1))
```

Production correction, 2026-04-14: this adapter surface is now quarantined in
`OSToWightmanPositivity.lean`.  The formal transitivity proof was fine as an
adapter, but the hypotheses of the supplier packet below it were too weak:
`hφf`/`hψg` alone are satisfied by the same-test specialization
`φ := f.1`, `ψ := g.1`.  Therefore this adapter may be revived only after the
supplier packet is restated with the explicit `(4.19)-(4.20)` transform
surface described above.  Until then, it is not an active production theorem
and should not be used as a downstream dependency.

Proof transcript:

1. Apply `lemma42_matrix_element_time_interchange` with
   `H := fun z =>
      bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShiftCanonicalExtension
        (d := d) OS lgc φ ψ hψ_compact z`.
2. Supply `hH_imag_os` from
   `bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShiftCanonicalExtension_imag_eq_osHolomorphicValue_of_fourierLaplaceTransform`.
3. Supply `hlimit` from
   `tendsto_bvt_F_canonical_xiShift_to_canonicalExtension_imagAxis_of_fourierLaplaceTransform`,
   proved from the Lemma-8.4 pointwise shell-limit supplier rather than from
   the withdrawn finite-height zero residual.

Quarantined status after the 2026-04-14 surface audit:

1. The formal dependency split remains useful: an hPsi packet would prove
   `hH_imag_os`, i.e. the identification of
   `bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShiftCanonicalExtension ... ((t : ℂ) * I)`
   with the OS holomorphic matrix element.  But the old hPsi theorem surface
   with only `hφf`/`hψg` is not live.
2. It does **not** supply the `hlimit` hypothesis of
   `lemma42_matrix_element_time_interchange`; that is supplied separately by

   `lemma84_bvt_W_timeShift_eq_integral_timeShift_mul_fourierTransform_psiZ_of_fourierLaplaceTransform`
   after the transform surface is in place.
3. The shell-limit theorem shape to recover, after the explicit transform

```lean
private theorem
    tendsto_bvt_F_canonical_xiShift_to_canonicalExtension_imagAxis_of_fourierLaplaceTransform
    -- explicit `(4.19)-(4.20)` transform hypotheses, not only `hφf`/`hψg`

```

   `tendsto_bvt_F_canonical_xiShift_conjTensorProduct_timeShift_boundaryValue`
   and

Readiness rule for this subsection:

1. No theorem without the explicit `(4.19)-(4.20)` transform surface may
   conclude a shell-to-canonical, real-time-to-Laplace, or Wightman-to-OS
   scalar identity.  In particular, `hφf`/`hψg` alone are insufficient.
2. Arbitrary-`φ, ψ` residual theorems may only compute obstruction limits; they
   must not be used as zero-limit targets.
3. As of the corrected 2026-04-13 readiness audit, the raw
   single-split/cutoff packet is retired. The support lemmas
   `tflat_pairing_eq_of_eqOn_dualCone`, `psiZ_twoPi_pairing_formula`, and
   `exists_flattened_bvt_F_dualCone_distribution_with_fourierLaplace_repr`
   have already been implemented and checked. The canonical positive-height
   shell packet and the horizontal Paley packet may be used only as diagnostic
   normal forms unless the explicit Section-4.3 transform hypotheses are
   present in the theorem statement.
4. The direct pointwise theorem
   `bvt_W_timeShift_sub_descendedPsiZ_zero_of_section43Transport` remains
   forbidden. The current shell-to-OS route must go through an explicit
   Fourier-Laplace transformed scalar bridge; the raw finite-height
   canonical-shell/horizontal dual-cone EqOn is withdrawn because it would
   identify a real-time oscillatory shell with an imaginary-axis Laplace
   damping factor for arbitrary `φ, ψ`.
5. Auxiliary Lean lemmas before the next production theorem are allowed only
   for the new `Section43FourierLaplaceTransform.lean` support packet, the
   transformed hPsi packet, or the transformed non-finite-height shell-limit
   supplier.  The already-implemented shell-side `Tflat` Fubini, horizontal
   Paley, and `ψ_Z` calculation lemmas may be cited as diagnostics, but they
   are not permission to implement another finite-height residual.
6. The hPsi compactness correction remains sound: use the direct off-diagonal
   helper
   `descendedPsiZ_boundaryValue_eq_selfAdjointSpectralLaplaceOffdiag_of_fourierLaplaceTransform`
   and never Wightman-side diagonal polarization. Any theorem that asks for
   `HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n → ℂ)` has left the live surface.
7. If Lean implementation exposes a genuine mathematical or type-level gap not
   covered by the displayed theorem slots, stop production edits and return to
   this proof-doc section first. Do not patch around the gap with wrappers or a
   weaker theorem shape.
8. After a non-finite-height shell-limit supplier is proved, feed it directly
   into `lemma42_matrix_element_time_interchange` together with the hPsi
   `hH_imag_os` identification. Do not route it through the withdrawn
   finite-height residual.
9. Optional downstream diagnostics involving raw `singleSplit` or
   `bvtSingleSplitXiShiftScalar` are not part of the live route. They may be
   revisited only after the main positivity path is closed or after a new
   valid tube representative is documented and checked.
10. Exact verification commands for the next Lean edits are:
    `lake env lean OSReconstruction/Wightman/Reconstruction/WickRotation/Section43FourierLaplaceTransform.lean`
    after implementing the support packet;
    `lake env lean OSReconstruction/Wightman/Reconstruction/WickRotation/OSToWightmanPositivity.lean`
    after implementing any hPsi theorem or non-finite-height shell-limit
    supplier; and
    `lake env lean OSReconstruction/Wightman/Reconstruction/WickRotation/OSToWightmanBoundaryValueLimits.lean`
    if a new support theorem is placed in BVLimits.
    If a support theorem is promoted from private to public in an imported
    file, run that exact support-file check first, then the downstream
    Positivity check. Do not replace these with a broad build.

### 5.9.5. Detailed proof of the final public closure

The final theorem

`bvt_W_positive_of_transportImage_density`

is proved in four formal stages:

1. Transport-image core.
   Apply `bvt_wightmanInner_eq_transport_norm_sq_onImage` and
   Hilbert-space nonnegativity on the transported image.

2. Hilbert-space density.
   Use the fact that the relevant positive-time vectors are dense in
   `OSHilbertSpace OS` by construction of the completion. This is the density
   actually used in the OS I positivity mechanism.

3. VEV/transport identification.
   Show that `bvt_W` agrees with the corresponding vacuum expectation value on
   the transported positive-time core.

4. Continuity closure.
   Use continuity of `bvt_W` together with Hilbert-space closure to pass from
   the transported core to arbitrary public `BorchersSequence d`.

The only continuity allowed here is the bounded finite-support continuity
already documented in the repo. Rebuilding theorem 3 from a new global topology
on raw `BorchersSequence d` remains off-route.

Current branch correction:

- the on-image transport package is real and already landed;
- the bounded finite-support continuity theorem is also now present in
  production, together with the exact closure reducer
  `bvt_wightmanInner_self_nonneg_of_bounded_onImage_approx` in
  `OSToWightmanPositivityOnImage.lean`;
- this is not merely heuristic: `Section43Codomain.not_denseRange_os1TransportComponent_succ`
  already proves the positive-degree non-density obstruction for
  `os1TransportComponent`, via evaluation at the boundary point `0`;
- the new theorem
  `not_exists_bounded_onImage_approx_of_component_apply_zero_ne` in
  `OSToWightmanPositivityOnImage.lean` now makes the raw closure obstruction
  explicit on the exact current theorem surface: the hypotheses of
  `bvt_wightmanInner_self_nonneg_of_bounded_onImage_approx` already force every
  positive-degree target component to vanish at `0`;
- therefore the public closure must still not be described as a density
  argument inside the Section-4.3 quotient codomain;
- accordingly, the remaining public frontier is **not** an arbitrary raw
  on-image supplier theorem;
- instead, the honest missing step is a prior VEV / boundary-value
  identification with three exact outputs:
  1. a boundary-vanishing surrogate `F₀`,
  2. equality of reconstructed quadratic forms between the original target and
     `F₀`,
  3. the zero-right and positive-degree transformed-image kernel inputs needed
     upstream of the existing closure reducer.

Exact current implementation status:

1. the old raw same-input theorem slogan is withdrawn and must not be
   implemented literally;
2. the current local production branch already contains the Stage-3/4
   transformed-image carrier and the on-image Hilbert transport;
3. the transformed-image quadratic identity has been reduced to the exact
   Stage-5 kernel inputs on the on-image carrier;
4. the live frontier is still the final positivity closure, but the exact
   current reducer only reaches targets whose positive-degree components vanish
   at the boundary point `0`;
5. this is already formalized on the existing positive-time side: the theorem
   `PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.component_apply_zero_eq_zero` in
   `OSToWightmanPositivityOnImage.lean` shows that every honest positive-time
   sequence satisfies the boundary-vanishing constraint degreewise;
6. therefore the blocker is not a generic on-image approximation supplier for
   arbitrary public `BorchersSequence d`, but the missing reduction that
   identifies a general public target with one satisfying that boundary-vanishing
   constraint, proves quadratic-form equality, and supplies the exact zero-right
   / positive-degree on-image kernel hypotheses before the reducer is invoked;
7. more sharply: after the new degreewise preimage constructor surface
   `BvtTransportImageSequence.ofPreimageFamily`, the first honest fixed-
   surrogate ingress theorem has now landed as
   `exists_bounded_componentwise_onImage_family_of_preimageFamilies` in
   `OSToWightmanPositivityOnImage.lean`; it shows that once an ambient
   approximating family already carries explicit degreewise positive-time
   preimages, the transformed-image family `G` together with its boundedness
   and componentwise convergence fields is purely formal;
8. on the canonical compact-family template
   `N ↦ PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.toBvtTransportImageSequence
        (compactApproxPositiveTimeBorchers F N)`, the carrier-side ambient
   compact-support field is now also source-backed: the theorem
   `PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.compactApproxPositiveTime_toBvtTransportImageSequence_hambientCompact`
   in `OSToWightmanPositivityOnImage.lean` proves the exact `hambientCompact`
   slot for that family;
9. after the latest compact-family pass, the same file now also proves the
   remaining canonical-family carrier-side fields `hprecompact` and `hG0`, and
   it contains the exact transitivity upgrade
   `compactApproxPositiveTime_timeShift_eq_osHolomorphicValue_of_singleSplit_eq_bvt_W_on_positiveReals`
   from the old compact-shell positive-real hypothesis to the corrected on-image
   `hreal` surface;
10. after the latest upstream ingress pass, the same file now also proves
    `exists_bounded_componentwise_onImage_supplier_data_of_preimageFamilies`;
    this is the exact fixed-surrogate supplier theorem available before `hreal`:
    given an ambient approximating family with explicit degreewise positive-time
    preimages, plus preimage-side compact support, ambient compact support, and
    degree-`0` vanishing, it packages `∃ G` together with the honest upstream
    fields `hG`, `hcomp`, `hprecompact`, `hambientCompact`, and `hG0`;
11. therefore the active upstream seam has narrowed again: below that
    source-backed supplier-data package, the still-missing payload is now
    exactly the positive-real-time comparison family `hreal`, not any of the
    other canonical-family carrier fields;
12. the first honest missing producer is now the earlier theorem that must
    actually manufacture this `hreal` payload from fixed-surrogate ambient /
    transformed-image data;
13. the minimal current source-backed target for that missing producer is not a
    new wrapper around `G`, but a comparison-layer theorem with the same
    preimage-family inputs already accepted by
    `exists_bounded_componentwise_onImage_supplier_data_of_preimageFamilies`:
    `F₀`, `m`, an ambient approximating family `F`, boundedness `hF`,
    componentwise convergence `hcomp`, explicit degreewise positive-time
    preimages `f`, transport identities `hf`, preimage compact support
    `hprecompact`, ambient compact support `hambientCompact`, and degree-`0`
    vanishing `hF0`;
14. the exact output of that theorem should be the positive-real family
    needed downstream to instantiate the current on-image supplier slot after
    the already-proved transitivity step:
    `∀ N n k, n ≤ (F N).bound → k ≤ (F N).bound → (hk_pos : 0 < k) →
       ∀ t : ℝ, 0 < t →
         bvt_W OS lgc (n + k)
           (((F N).funcs n).conjTensorProduct
             (timeShiftSchwartzNPoint (d := d) t ((F N).funcs k))) =
         OSInnerProductTimeShiftHolomorphicValue (d := d) OS lgc
           (PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.single n
             (((f N n :
                 euclideanPositiveTimeSubmodule (d := d) n) :
               SchwartzNPoint d n))
             ((f N n).2))
           (PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.single k
             (((f N k :
                 euclideanPositiveTimeSubmodule (d := d) k) :
               SchwartzNPoint d k))
             ((f N k).2)) (t : ℂ)`;
15. equivalently, one may first prove the same theorem with
    `bvt_singleSplit_xiShiftHolomorphicValue ... (t : ℂ)` on the left and then
    compose with
    `compactApproxPositiveTime_timeShift_eq_osHolomorphicValue_of_singleSplit_eq_bvt_W_on_positiveReals`
    or its fixed-surrogate analogue once that transitivity surface is exposed;
16. until that upstream producer exists, the full supplier payload
    `∃ G, hG, hcomp, hprecompact, hambientCompact, hG0, hreal`
    still cannot be recovered from the present source graph merely by composing
    the already-landed consumer cluster;
17. the theorem-3 blueprint now endorses only the transformed-image /
    Hilbert-density reading of Section 4.3.

## 6. Exact relation to the current production consumers

The current legacy consumers can remain compiled, but they are not part of the
endorsed closure route.

Safe usage:

1. mine the current codebase only for reusable continuity / completion /
   positive-time Hilbert-space facts;
2. keep the deprecated `hschw` consumer chain compiled but untouched;
3. if a legacy theorem becomes useful later, re-justify it from the corrected
   transformed-image Package-I route rather than treating it as an input.

Unsafe usage:

1. introducing a new theorem whose primary conclusion is another `h*` consumer
   interface;
2. reentering the K2 common-kernel route;
3. implementing the old raw Package-I theorem surface on the same
   `BorchersSequence d` input without first fixing the transformed-image
   statement.

## 7. Minimal Lean pseudocode for the endorsed route

The later Lean implementation should use the following theorem-package names
unless an exact compile failure forces a local renaming. In that case the docs
must be updated at the same time; the names below are now part of the intended
implementation contract, not casual pseudocode.

```lean
/- Step A: one-variable identity theorem. -/
theorem identity_theorem_right_halfplane ... := by
  ...

/- Step B: compact positive-time single/split equality on {Re z > 0}. -/
theorem bvt_xiShift_eq_osInnerProduct_holomorphicValue_single ... := by
  apply identity_theorem_right_halfplane
  ...

/- Step C: withdrawn same-shell boundary pairing route (historical, false). -/
-- Do not implement `schwinger_timeShift_eq_bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShift`.
-- The same-input shell identity is false and remains quarantined.

/- Step D: withdrawn raw positive-time sesquilinear route (historical, false). -/
-- Do not implement `bvt_wightmanInner_eq_osInner_of_positiveTime`.

/- Step E: withdrawn raw positive-time positivity route (historical, false). -/
-- Do not implement `bvt_positiveTime_self_nonneg_from_packageC`.

/- Step F: withdrawn raw density route (historical, do not implement). -/
theorem orderedPositiveTime_dense_schwartzNPoint ... := by
  ...

theorem exists_orderedPositiveTime_seq_tendsto ... := by
  ...

/- Step G: withdrawn approximation bundling route (historical, do not implement). -/
theorem exists_positiveTime_componentwise_approximation ... := by
  ...

/- Step H: withdrawn continuity-after-density route (historical, do not implement). -/
theorem WightmanInnerProduct_tendsto_of_componentwise_tendsto ... := by
  ...

theorem re_WightmanInnerProduct_tendsto_of_componentwise_tendsto ... := by
  ...

/- Step I: final theorem-3 closure via Section 4.3 transformed image. -/
def bvtTransportImage ... := by
  ...

def BvtTransportImageSequence ... := by
  ...

noncomputable def bvt_transport_to_osHilbert_onImage ... := by
  ...

theorem bvt_wightmanInner_eq_transport_norm_sq_onImage ... := by
  ...

theorem bvt_W_positive_of_transportImage_density ... := by
  ...
```

The current production file
`OSReconstruction/Wightman/Reconstruction/WickRotation/OSToWightmanPositivity.lean`
already uses the exact names
`identity_theorem_right_halfplane`,
`bvt_xiShift_eq_osInnerProduct_holomorphicValue_single`.
The transformed-image theorem names above should therefore be treated as the
fixed guidance for the corrected Section 4.3 closure package. The older raw
same-input names
`schwinger_timeShift_eq_bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShift`,
`bvt_wightmanInner_eq_osInner_of_positiveTime`,
and `bvt_positiveTime_self_nonneg_from_packageC`
are withdrawn historical names and should not be reintroduced as theorem
targets.

## 8. Representation-gap note

The theorem-3 route above is the current-code realization of the OS proof.

The docs therefore record the following explicitly:

1. raw `BorchersSequence d` is a bounded finite-record representation;
2. this representation is enough for theorem 3 because
   `WightmanInnerProduct` is a finite sum;
3. therefore theorem 3 should be closed by the Section 4.3 transport-map
   realization, not by first imposing a `Submodule` / topology package on the
   raw type.

This is a local repo encoding fact, not a mathematical change to the OS route.

## 9. Support-work checklist

Before any later production proof resumes, the support work should verify:

1. no theorem-3 doc section still treats `hschw` as the target theorem shape;
2. no theorem-3 doc section still recommends `bvtInitialCore` on raw
   `BorchersSequence d`;
3. `mathlib_gap_analysis.md` classifies the raw `BorchersSequence` issue as a
   local representation gap, not an upstream gap;
4. any new test-file support work stays inside Package A through Package I
   above, and does not revive the withdrawn F/G/H density route as if it were
   endorsed.
5. the docs identify the exact current local frontier as the corrected
   transformed-image Package-I theorem surface, not as a generic
   “boundary-value limit”.
6. the docs explicitly record that Packages A-B are already implemented in
   `OSToWightmanPositivity.lean`;
7. no theorem-3 doc section still states the naive raw theorem
   `WightmanInnerProduct(bvt_W)(F,F).re = ‖u(F)‖^2` on the same raw public
   `BorchersSequence d` input as if it were already the correct Section 4.3
   theorem surface.

## 10. Bottom line

Theorem 3 is no longer a shell-comparison project.

Theorem 3 is now exactly this:

1. prove the positive-time semigroup bridge;
2. prove positivity on `PositiveTimeBorchersSequence d`;
3. define the Section 4.3 transformed Minkowski image and its OS Hilbert-space
   transport map;
4. identify the Wightman quadratic form with a Hilbert norm square on that
   transformed-image core;
5. extend positivity from the transformed-image core to arbitrary
   `BorchersSequence d` by density/continuity.

That is the only theorem-3 route this note now endorses.

  existential constructor:

```lean
private theorem fixedPair_left_descended_psiZ_boundaryValue_noCompact
    (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d) (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
    {n m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
    (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n) (ψ : SchwartzNPoint d m) :
    ∃ Tφψ : SchwartzMap ℝ ℂ →L[ℂ] ℂ,
      SCV.HasOneSidedFourierSupport Tφψ ∧
      ∀ t : ℝ, 0 < t →
        bvt_W OS lgc (n + m)
          (φ.conjTensorProduct (timeShiftSchwartzNPoint (d := d) t ψ)) =
        OSReconstruction.fourierPairingDescendsToSection43PositiveEnergy1D
          Tφψ
          ‹SCV.HasOneSidedFourierSupport Tφψ›
          (section43PositiveEnergyQuotientMap1D
            (SCV.schwartzPsiZ (((2 * Real.pi : ℂ) * (t * Complex.I))) ...))
```

  equivalently, one may bind the support witness explicitly as
  `∃ Tφψ, ∃ hTφψ_supp, ∀ t > 0, ...`;
- this theorem is still not implementation-ready from current source because no
  existing compact-support-free constructor produces the full fixed-pair
  `Tφψ`/support package together with the required `ψ_{2πit}` value family;
  however, the first hidden ingredient strictly below it has now been exposed
  and landed in
  `OSToWightmanBoundaryValueLimits.lean`:
  `continuous_bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShift_noCompact`
  and, using it,
  `exists_bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShift_temperedFunctional_noCompact`;
- these new theorems give, for an arbitrary fixed pair `(φ, ψ)`, an honest
  compact-support-free continuous linear functional
  `T : SchwartzMap ℝ ℂ →L[ℂ] ℂ` with
  `SCV.HasOneSidedFourierSupport T` and
  `T χ = ∫ t, bvt_W(... (φ.conjTensorProduct (timeShiftSchwartzNPoint t ψ))) * χ t`;
- what still does **not** exist on the current branch is the next upgrade from
  that raw no-compact functional package to the sharpened fixed-pair
  `ψ_{2πit}` existential family
  `∃ Tφψ, ∃ hTφψ_supp, ∀ t > 0, ...`;
- therefore the exact first remaining hidden ingredient beneath the sharpened
  fixed-pair existential theorem is no longer raw tempered-functional
  existence itself, but the theorem that upgrades
  `exists_bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShift_temperedFunctional_noCompact`
  into the actual `ψ_{2πit}`-specialized fixed-pair existential constructor;
- this sharpened fixed-pair existential theorem remains the next honest
  theorem-sized target beneath `ambient_descended_psiZ_boundaryValue_eq_fixedPair`.
- only after that left-hand compact-support-free `ψ_{2πit}` specialization
  exists does the
  previous piece
  `ambient_descended_psiZ_boundaryValue_eq_fixedPair`
  become a meaningful transport-backed assembly/comparison target.

Landed theorem surface:

```lean
private theorem
    bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShift_noCompact_psiZ_eq_osHolomorphicValue_of_ambient_descended_boundaryValue_eq
    (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d) (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
    {n m : ℕ}
    (f : SchwartzNPoint d n)
    (hf_ord : tsupport (f : NPointDomain d n → ℂ) ⊆ OrderedPositiveTimeRegion d n)
    (g : SchwartzNPoint d m)
    (hg_ord : tsupport (g : NPointDomain d m → ℂ) ⊆ OrderedPositiveTimeRegion d m)
    (Tφψ : SchwartzMap ℝ ℂ →L[ℂ] ℂ)
    (hTφψ_supp : SCV.HasOneSidedFourierSupport Tφψ)
    (hPsi :
      let xF : OSHilbertSpace OS := (((show OSPreHilbertSpace OS from
        (⟦PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.single n f hf_ord⟧)) : OSHilbertSpace OS))
      let xG : OSHilbertSpace OS := (((show OSPreHilbertSpace OS from
        (⟦PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.single m g hg_ord⟧)) : OSHilbertSpace OS))
      ∀ t : ℝ, ∀ ht : 0 < t,
        OSReconstruction.fourierPairingDescendsToSection43PositiveEnergy1D
          Tφψ hTφψ_supp
          (section43PositiveEnergyQuotientMap1D
            (SCV.schwartzPsiZ (((2 * Real.pi : ℂ) * (t * Complex.I))) ...)) =
        selfAdjointSpectralBoundaryValueOffdiagCLM
          (osTimeShiftHilbert (d := d) OS lgc 1 one_pos)
          (osTimeShiftHilbert_isSelfAdjoint (d := d) OS lgc 1 one_pos)
          xF xG
          (SCV.schwartzPsiZ (((2 * Real.pi : ℂ) * (t * Complex.I))) ...)) :
    ∀ t : ℝ, ∀ ht : 0 < t,
      Tφψ
        ((SchwartzMap.fourierTransformCLM ℂ)
          (SCV.schwartzPsiZ (((2 * Real.pi : ℂ) * (t * Complex.I))) ...)) =
      OSInnerProductTimeShiftHolomorphicValue (d := d) OS lgc
        (PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.single n f hf_ord)
        (PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.single m g hg_ord) (t : ℂ)
```

  `exists_bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShift_temperedFunctional_noCompact_psiZ`.

```lean
private theorem ambient_descended_psiZ_boundaryValue_eq_fixedPair
    (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d) (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
    {n m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
    (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n) (ψ : SchwartzNPoint d m)
    (f : SchwartzNPoint d n)
    (hf_ord : tsupport (f : NPointDomain d n → ℂ) ⊆ OrderedPositiveTimeRegion d n)
    (g : SchwartzNPoint d m)
    (hg_ord : tsupport (g : NPointDomain d m → ℂ) ⊆ OrderedPositiveTimeRegion d m)
    (hφf :
      section43PositiveEnergyQuotientMap (d := d) n φ =
        os1TransportComponent d n ⟨f, hf_ord⟩)
    (hψg :
      section43PositiveEnergyQuotientMap (d := d) m ψ =
        os1TransportComponent d m ⟨g, hg_ord⟩) :
    let xF : OSHilbertSpace OS := (((show OSPreHilbertSpace OS from
      (⟦PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.single n f hf_ord⟧)) : OSHilbertSpace OS))
    let xG : OSHilbertSpace OS := (((show OSPreHilbertSpace OS from
      (⟦PositiveTimeBorchersSequence.single m g hg_ord⟧)) : OSHilbertSpace OS))
    let Tφψ : SchwartzMap ℝ ℂ →L[ℂ] ℂ :=
      Classical.choose
        (exists_bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShift_temperedFunctional_noCompact_psiZ
          (d := d) OS lgc hm φ ψ)
    ∀ t : ℝ, ∀ ht : 0 < t,
      Tφψ
        ((SchwartzMap.fourierTransformCLM ℂ)
          (SCV.schwartzPsiZ
            (((2 * Real.pi : ℂ) * (t * Complex.I)))
            (by
              simpa [Complex.mul_im, ht.ne']
                using mul_pos Real.two_pi_pos ht))) =
      selfAdjointSpectralBoundaryValueOffdiagCLM
        (osTimeShiftHilbert (d := d) OS lgc 1 one_pos)
        (osTimeShiftHilbert_isSelfAdjoint (d := d) OS lgc 1 one_pos)
        xF xG
        (SCV.schwartzPsiZ
          (((2 * Real.pi : ℂ) * (t * Complex.I)))
          (by
            simpa [Complex.mul_im, ht.ne']
              using mul_pos Real.two_pi_pos ht))
```

- therefore the exact first lower ingredient strictly below

```lean
private theorem ambient_noCompact_witness_apply_horizontalPsiZ_fixedPair
    (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d) (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
    {n m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
    (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n) (ψ : SchwartzNPoint d m) :
    let Tφψ : SchwartzMap ℝ ℂ →L[ℂ] ℂ :=
      Classical.choose
        (exists_bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShift_temperedFunctional_noCompact_psiZ
          (d := d) OS lgc hm φ ψ)
    ∀ ε : ℝ, ∀ hε : 0 < ε, ∀ x : ℝ,
      Tφψ
        ((SchwartzMap.fourierTransformCLM ℂ)
          (SCV.schwartzPsiZ
            ((((2 * Real.pi : ℝ) : ℂ) * ((x : ℂ) + ε * Complex.I)))
            (by
              have hscaled : 0 < (2 * Real.pi) * (((x : ℂ) + ε * Complex.I).im) :=
                mul_pos Real.two_pi_pos (by simpa using hε)
              simpa [Complex.mul_im] using hscaled))) =
      ∫ τ : ℝ,
        bvt_W OS lgc (n + m)
          (φ.conjTensorProduct (timeShiftSchwartzNPoint (d := d) τ ψ)) *
        (SchwartzMap.fourierTransformCLM ℂ
          (SCV.schwartzPsiZ
            ((((2 * Real.pi : ℝ) : ℂ) * ((x : ℂ) + ε * Complex.I)))
            (by
              have hscaled : 0 < (2 * Real.pi) * (((x : ℂ) + ε * Complex.I).im) :=
                mul_pos Real.two_pi_pos (by simpa using hε)
              simpa [Complex.mul_im] using hscaled))) τ
```

- minimal exact package statement required on the current branch:

```lean
private theorem
    exists_bvt_W_conjTensorProduct_timeShift_temperedFunctional_noCompact_psiZ
    (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d) (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
    {n m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
    (f : SchwartzNPoint d n)
    (g : SchwartzNPoint d m) :
    ∃ T : SchwartzMap ℝ ℂ →L[ℂ] ℂ,
      ∃ hT_supp : SCV.HasOneSidedFourierSupport T,
        (∀ χ : SchwartzMap ℝ ℂ,
          T χ =
            ∫ τ : ℝ,
              bvt_W OS lgc (n + m)
                (f.conjTensorProduct
                  (timeShiftSchwartzNPoint (d := d) τ g)) * χ τ) ∧
        ∀ t : ℝ, ∀ ht : 0 < t,
          T
            ((SchwartzMap.fourierTransformCLM ℂ)
              (SCV.schwartzPsiZ
                (((2 * Real.pi : ℂ) * (t * Complex.I)))
                (by
                  simpa [Complex.mul_im, ht.ne']
                    using mul_pos Real.two_pi_pos ht))) =
          OSReconstruction.fourierPairingDescendsToSection43PositiveEnergy1D
            T hT_supp
            (OSReconstruction.section43PositiveEnergyQuotientMap1D
              (SCV.schwartzPsiZ
                (((2 * Real.pi : ℂ) * (t * Complex.I)))
                (by
                  simpa [Complex.mul_im, ht.ne']
                    using mul_pos Real.two_pi_pos ht)))
```

```lean
private theorem section43_fixedPair_bvtW_timeShift_eq_osHolomorphicValue_fixedTime
    (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d) (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
    {n m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
    (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n) (ψ : SchwartzNPoint d m)
    (f : SchwartzNPoint d n)
```

Addendum (2026-04-14, weaker-than-global repair audit):

  1. the smallest weaker-than-global theorem surface that would be directly
     usable after `have hweightEqOnSource := ...`, namely the actual-shell
     limit-level comparison

```lean
Filter.Tendsto
  (fun ε : ℝ =>
    ∫ y : NPointDomain d (n + m),
      bvt_F OS lgc (n + m)
        (xiShift ⟨n, Nat.lt_add_of_pos_right hm⟩ 0
          (fun k μ =>
            ↑(y k μ) +

                Complex.I)
          (t : ℂ)) *
        (((φ.conjTensorProduct ψ) - (f.conjTensorProduct g)) y))
  (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0))
  (nhds 0),
```

     equivalently equality of the two shell limits against

     for arbitrary shell points;

Addendum (2026-04-14, bounded quotient-descent insertion-point audit):

- tested the sharpened quotient-descent theorem surface for fixed `t > 0`:

```lean
∀ h : SchwartzNPoint d (n + m),
  section43PositiveEnergyQuotientMap (d := d) (n + m) h = 0 →
    Filter.Tendsto
      (fun ε : ℝ =>
        ∫ y : NPointDomain d (n + m),
          bvt_F OS lgc (n + m)
            (xiShift ⟨n, Nat.lt_add_of_pos_right hm⟩ 0
              (fun k μ =>
                ↑(y k μ) +
                  ε * ↑(canonicalForwardConeDirection (d := d) (n + m) k μ) *
                    Complex.I)
              (t : ℂ)) *
            h y)
      (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0))
      (nhds 0)
```

- re-reading `Section43Codomain.lean` shows the existing descended-functional
  package is still too small for this move:
  the only relevant `liftQ` descendants currently present are the multivariate
  boundary map `evalAtZeroDescendsToSection43PositiveEnergyComponent` and the
  1D half-line descended functionals
  `fourierPairingDescendsToSection43PositiveEnergy1D` /
  `evalAtZeroDescendsToSection43PositiveEnergy1D`;
- no theorem yet packages the ambient fixed-time shell integral as a
  continuous linear functional on `SchwartzNPoint d (n + m)`, and no kernel
  theorem shows that this shell functional annihilates
  `section43PositiveEnergyVanishingSubmodule (d := d) (n + m)`;
- bounded functional-packaging recheck on the current branch sharpens that
  verdict:
  at the generic Schwartz-space level, the raw fixed-`ε` shell integral could
  in principle be bundled as a `ContinuousLinearMap` by the same existing
  pattern used in
  `OSToWightmanPositivity.lean` for `fourierInvPairingCLM` and
  `horizontalPhasePairingCLM`, namely
  `SchwartzMap.smulLeftCLM` followed by `SchwartzMap.integralCLM`;
  but there is still no already-landed theorem with the exact shell coefficient

  `y ↦ bvt_F OS lgc (n + m)
      (xiShift ⟨n, Nat.lt_add_of_pos_right hm⟩ 0
        (fun k μ =>
          ↑(y k μ) +
            ε * ↑(canonicalForwardConeDirection (d := d) (n + m) k μ) *
              Complex.I)
        (t : ℂ))`

  proved as `Function.HasTemperateGrowth`, and no existing definition in the
  repo packages the resulting multivariate shell functional by name;
  the only nearby growth theorem is the unbundled estimate
  `bvt_F_hasGlobalForwardTubeGrowth`, which is not yet turned into a named
  shell-coefficient temperate-growth API;
- therefore the quotient-descent theorem above is itself the first missing
  descent theorem on the current branch, not a formal consequence of the
  existing codomain API;
- continuing the 2026-04-14 quotient-kernel pass after the Entry #511
  tightening, the earliest honest fixed-`ε` restatement of this seam is the
  per-shell kernel contract

```lean
theorem section43_fixedTime_shellIntegral_eq_zero_of_quotient_eq_zero
    (t ε : ℝ) (ht : 0 < t) (hε : 0 < ε) :
    ∀ h : SchwartzNPoint d (n + m),
      section43PositiveEnergyQuotientMap (d := d) (n + m) h = 0 →
        ∫ y : NPointDomain d (n + m),
          bvt_F OS lgc (n + m)
            (xiShift ⟨n, Nat.lt_add_of_pos_right hm⟩ 0
              (fun k μ =>
                ↑(y k μ) +
                  ε * ↑(canonicalForwardConeDirection (d := d) (n + m) k μ) *
                    Complex.I)
              (t : ℂ)) *
            h y = 0
```

- this fixed-`ε` formulation is not genuinely smaller than the already-recorded
  limit theorem
  `section43_fixedTime_shellLimit_tendsto_zero_of_quotient_eq_zero`;
  source-first, both statements ask for the same missing mathematics:
  complement-localization of the multivariate fixed-time shell functional on
  arbitrary ambient `h`, equivalently kernel containment
  `section43PositiveEnergyVanishingSubmodule (d := d) (n + m) ⊆ ker(shell)`;
- no existing non-wrapper theorem on the current branch proves that stronger
  ambient trimming statement, even under an explicit complement-vanishing
  hypothesis on a general ambient test function;
- `section43_iteratedSlice_descendedPairing` remains downstream of this seam:
  it starts only after frozen-slice reduction to the 1D quotient and so cannot
  justify the ambient shell-limit descent required in `hlimit_os`;
- exact first missing proof ingredient remains:
  a theorem that the fixed-time shell-limit functional annihilates
  `section43PositiveEnergyVanishingSubmodule (d := d) (n + m)`.

  shell-functional theorem surface on the present branch;
- reason: once explicit complement vanishing is assumed, the fixed-pair
  packaging adds only provenance for that particular `h`, not any new theorem
  that descends the ambient fixed-time shell integral or its `ε → 0+` limit
  through the Section-4.3 quotient;

Addendum (2026-04-14, bounded derivative-growth package audit):

- source-first the forward-tube-to-shell packaging order is now explicit:
  1. `bvt_F_holomorphic` in
     `OSToWightmanBoundaryValuesBase.lean` gives
     `DifferentiableOn ℂ (bvt_F OS lgc (n + m)) (ForwardTube d (n + m))`;
  2. `canonicalXiShift_mem_forwardTube` in
     `OSToWightmanPositivity.lean` gives range inclusion of the fixed-time
     affine shell map

     `y ↦ xiShift ⟨n, Nat.lt_add_of_pos_right hm⟩ 0
        (fun k μ =>
          ↑(y k μ) +
            ε * ↑(canonicalForwardConeDirection (d := d) (n + m) k μ) *
              Complex.I)
        (t : ℂ)`

     into `ForwardTube d (n + m)` for `ε > 0`;
  3. `contDiff_canonicalXiShift` in
     `OSToWightmanPositivity.lean` now packages that affine shell map as
     `ContDiff ℝ (⊤ : ℕ∞)`;
  4. `isOpen_forwardTube` together with
     `DifferentiableOn.contDiffOn` from
     `Mathlib/Analysis/Complex/CauchyIntegral.lean` therefore leaves the
     smoothness half of the shell-coefficient target source-backed in
     principle;
  5. `bvt_F_hasGlobalForwardTubeGrowth` gives the tube-level zeroth-order bound
     on values of `bvt_F`;
  6. `bvt_F_canonicalXiShift_hasPolynomialGrowth` in
     `OSToWightmanPositivity.lean` now specializes that to the actual
     fixed-time shell coefficient, i.e. the `r = 0` polynomial-growth case on
     `NPointDomain d (n + m)`.
- exact first missing theorem contract in that package is now sharpened to the
  positive-order derivative-growth half alone:

```lean
theorem section43_fixedTime_shellCoefficient_iteratedFDeriv_polynomialBound
    (t ε : ℝ) (ht : 0 < t) (hε : 0 < ε) :
    ∀ r : ℕ, 1 ≤ r → ∃ k : ℕ, ∃ C : ℝ, 0 ≤ C ∧
      ∀ y : NPointDomain d (n + m),
        ‖iteratedFDeriv ℝ r
            (fun y : NPointDomain d (n + m) =>
              bvt_F OS lgc (n + m)
                (xiShift ⟨n, Nat.lt_add_of_pos_right hm⟩ 0
                  (fun k μ =>
                    ↑(y k μ) +
                      ε * ↑(canonicalForwardConeDirection (d := d) (n + m) k μ) *
                        Complex.I)
                  (t : ℂ))) y‖
          ≤ C * (1 + ‖y‖) ^ k
```

- once that theorem exists, the final shell-coefficient
  `Function.HasTemperateGrowth` theorem is a packaging step; before it, the
  obstruction is no longer vague and is not the shell-map smoothness or the
  `r = 0` growth case.

- current branch/source recheck confirms there is still no honest already-landed
  theorem or Mathlib API that upgrades the above package from
  forward-tube holomorphy + affine-shell smoothness + the shell-specialized
  `r = 0` bound to the positive-order derivative-growth theorem contract;
- exact source-backed ingredients present locally are:
  1. `bvt_F_holomorphic` for `DifferentiableOn ℂ` on `ForwardTube d (n + m)`;
  2. `canonicalXiShift_mem_forwardTube` for range inclusion of the fixed-time
     affine shell map into `ForwardTube d (n + m)`;
  3. `contDiff_canonicalXiShift` for domain-side `ContDiff ℝ ∞`;
  4. `DifferentiableOn.contDiffOn` plus `isOpen_forwardTube` for the
     smoothness-only restriction of `bvt_F` to the shell image;
  5. `bvt_F_hasGlobalForwardTubeGrowth` for tube-level zeroth-order value
     growth;
  6. `bvt_F_canonicalXiShift_hasPolynomialGrowth` for the shell-specialized
     zeroth-order bound.
- exact Mathlib APIs checked in this pass and found insufficient for the next
  step:
  1. `AnalyticOnNhd.iteratedFDeriv_of_isOpen` and `AnalyticOn.contDiffOn`
     propagate analyticity / smoothness, but do not produce polynomial growth
     bounds for derivatives;
  2. `Complex.norm_iteratedDeriv_le_of_forall_mem_sphere_norm_le` and the
     circle-integral formulas are one-variable estimates and do not supply the
     needed multivariate shell theorem out of the box;
  3. `norm_iteratedFDeriv_comp_le'` and `Function.HasTemperateGrowth.comp'`
     are downstream composition tools that require derivative bounds for the
     outer function already in hand.
- therefore no honest intermediate theorem below
  `section43_fixedTime_shellCoefficient_iteratedFDeriv_polynomialBound`
  was already available except wrapper-only repackaging, so this bounded pass
  makes no production Lean edit.

Addendum (2026-04-14, bounded OS-route supervision pass after Entry #659):

- source-first recheck against the exact current branch state confirms the
  derivative-growth package order is unchanged and honest:
  `bvt_F_holomorphic`
  -> `canonicalXiShift_mem_forwardTube`
  -> `contDiff_canonicalXiShift`
  -> `DifferentiableOn.contDiffOn` on `isOpen_forwardTube`
  -> `bvt_F_hasGlobalForwardTubeGrowth`
  -> `bvt_F_canonicalXiShift_hasPolynomialGrowth`;
- the `DifferentiableOn.contDiffOn` step is only a smoothness bridge for the
  outer holomorphic function on the shell image; it does not supply growth for
  any positive-order `iteratedFDeriv ℝ r`;
- the local Mathlib APIs rechecked in this supervision pass remain
  insufficient in exactly the same way:
  `AnalyticOnNhd.iteratedFDeriv_of_isOpen` and `AnalyticOn.contDiffOn`
  propagate analyticity / smoothness, the complex Cauchy/Liouville estimates
  are one-variable only, and
  `norm_iteratedFDeriv_comp_le'` / `Function.HasTemperateGrowth.comp'` are
  usable only after outer derivative-growth bounds already exist;
- therefore the exact first missing positive-order theorem contract is still
  the shell-coefficient estimate
  `section43_fixedTime_shellCoefficient_iteratedFDeriv_polynomialBound`,
  equivalently:

```lean
∀ r : ℕ, 1 ≤ r → ∃ k : ℕ, ∃ C : ℝ, 0 ≤ C ∧
  ∀ y : NPointDomain d (n + m),
    ‖iteratedFDeriv ℝ r
        (fun y : NPointDomain d (n + m) =>
          bvt_F OS lgc (n + m)
            (xiShift ⟨n, Nat.lt_add_of_pos_right hm⟩ 0
              (fun k μ =>
                ↑(y k μ) +
                  ε * ↑(canonicalForwardConeDirection (d := d) (n + m) k μ) *
                    Complex.I)
              (t : ℂ))) y‖
      ≤ C * (1 + ‖y‖) ^ k
```

- no smaller honest non-wrapper theorem below that contract was found already
  landed on this branch, so this supervision pass makes no production Lean
  edit.

- source-first recheck of the repo-local analytic files actually relevant to
  this seam
  `SCV/Analyticity.lean`,
  `SCV/IteratedCauchyIntegral.lean`,
  `SCV/TubeBoundaryValueExistence.lean`,
  together with the already-imported local path

  -> `OSToWightmanPositivity.lean`,
  found no honest reusable theorem turning multivariate holomorphy on an open
  complex domain plus polynomial value growth into even the first positive-order
  polynomial bound for the fixed-time affine shell coefficient;
- exact already-landed repo-local analytic package order for that shell
  coefficient is therefore still:
  `full_analytic_continuation_with_symmetry_growth`
  -> `bvt_F_holomorphic`
  -> `canonicalXiShift_mem_forwardTube`
  -> `contDiff_canonicalXiShift`
  -> `DifferentiableOn.contDiffOn` on `isOpen_forwardTube`
  -> `bvt_F_hasGlobalForwardTubeGrowth`
  -> `bvt_F_canonicalXiShift_hasPolynomialGrowth`;
- the exact derivative-growth consumer in the SCV path is now source-checked
  more sharply:
  in `SCV/TubeBoundaryValueExistence.lean`, the only place where derivative
  growth is consumed is the call to
  `hasDerivAt_schwartz_integral` inside
  `hasDerivAt_tubeSlice_ray`;
  the downstream theorems
  `continuous_tubeSlice_ray_deriv` and
  `cr_integration_identity`
  use that result, but do not ask for a stronger derivative-growth package;
- the contract consumed there is strictly smaller than a full multivariate
  shell theorem of the form
  `iteratedFDeriv ℝ 1 ...` with global polynomial growth:
  the proof only needs a local-in-`τ` ray-direction bound for the one-variable
  derivative integrand

  `Fparam' τ x := (fderiv ℂ F (slice τ x)) vη`;

  more exactly, the consumed hypothesis is

```lean
∃ (C_bd' : ℝ) (N' : ℕ), 0 < C_bd' ∧
  ∀ τ, |τ - τ₀| < δ → ∀ x,
    ‖(fderiv ℂ F (slice τ x)) vη‖ ≤ C_bd' * (1 + ‖x‖) ^ N'
```

  where
  `slice τ x i = (x i : ℂ) + ((τ • η) i : ℝ) * Complex.I`
  and
  `vη i = (η i : ℂ) * Complex.I`;
- source-first, `SCV/TubeBoundaryValueExistence.lean` proves that smaller
  contract internally by a one-complex-variable Cauchy estimate on
  `w ↦ F (x + w η I)`;
  but the current repo still does not expose an already-landed reusable theorem
  specializing that local estimate to the theorem-3 fixed-time shell
  coefficient, and this pass did not leave one in production;
- exact import/dependency-surface verdict after checking
  `SCV/TubeBoundaryValueExistence.lean`,
  `SCV/TubeBoundaryValues.lean`,
  `OSToWightmanBoundaryValuesBase.lean`,
  and `OSToWightmanPositivity.lean`:
  the first honest place where that sharpened local ray-derivative-growth
  contract could live is upstream in
  `SCV/TubeBoundaryValueExistence.lean`, immediately at the consumer seam above
  `hasDerivAt_tubeSlice_ray`, because that file already contains the full local
  Cauchy-estimate proof internally and the later SCV theorems only consume that
  result;
- however, that place is not a current compile-clean insertion point for this
  branch:
  theorem 3 imports
  `OSToWightmanBoundaryValuesBase.lean`, which in turn imports the abstract
  boundary-value layer `SCV/TubeBoundaryValues.lean` rather than the concrete

  `lake env lean` on the present branch;
- so there is no honest compile-clean theorem landing available in

```lean
theorem section43_fixedTime_shellIntegral_eq_zero_of_quotient_eq_zero
    (t ε : ℝ) (ht : 0 < t) (hε : 0 < ε) :
    ∀ h : SchwartzNPoint d (n + m),
      section43PositiveEnergyQuotientMap (d := d) (n + m) h = 0 →

              (fun k μ =>

                    Complex.I)

            h y = 0
```

```lean
theorem section43_fixedTime_shellLimit_tendsto_zero_of_quotient_eq_zero
    (t : ℝ) (ht : 0 < t) :
    ∀ h : SchwartzNPoint d (n + m),
      section43PositiveEnergyQuotientMap (d := d) (n + m) h = 0 →
        Filter.Tendsto
          (fun ε : ℝ =>
            ∫ y : NPointDomain d (n + m),
              bvt_F OS lgc (n + m)
                (xiShift ⟨n, Nat.lt_add_of_pos_right hm⟩ 0
                  (fun k μ =>
                    ↑(y k μ) +
                      ε * ↑(canonicalForwardConeDirection (d := d) (n + m) k μ) *
                        Complex.I)
                  (t : ℂ)) *
                h y)
          (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0))
          (nhds 0)
```

- the missing quotient-descent theorem is now best treated as a four-piece
  package, not as a vague single black box;
- Piece 1: define the fixed-`ε` shell integral as the raw continuous linear
  functional on `SchwartzNPoint d (n + m)` obtained by pairing `h` against the
  fixed-time shell weight
  `y ↦ bvt_F OS lgc (n + m) (xiShift ... (fun k μ => ↑(y k μ) + ε * ... * I) (t : ℂ))`;
- Piece 2: prove the genuine new kernel theorem for that raw functional,
  namely that its kernel contains
  `section43PositiveEnergyVanishingSubmodule (d := d) (n + m)`;
- Piece 3: descend the raw functional through
  `section43PositiveEnergyQuotientMap (d := d) (n + m)` by the same `liftQ`
  pattern already used in `Section43Codomain.lean` for
  `evalAtZeroDescendsToSection43PositiveEnergyComponent` and the one-variable
  `fourierPairingDescendsToSection43PositiveEnergy1D`;
- Piece 4: apply the descended functional to
  `h := (φ.conjTensorProduct ψ) - (f.conjTensorProduct g)` using the already
  available quotient-zero transport
  `section43PositiveEnergyQuotientMap_conjTensorProduct_eq_of_repr_eq_transport`,
  then pass from fixed `ε` equality to the `ε → 0+` limit statement;
- current source status: Pieces 1, 3, and 4 look like packaging/interfaces,

  theorem proves it even for the live fixed-pair difference.

Addendum (2026-04-14, bounded theorem-3 actual-shell supervision pass: first strict sub-`of_fderiv` surface):

- exact stricter source-first sharpening below full

  the smallest honest theorem/local-lemma surface on the live shell route was

  the zeroth-order polynomial bound for the derivative-valued coefficient
  itself, namely

```lean
∃ (C_bd : ℝ) (N : ℕ), 0 < C_bd ∧
  ∀ y : NPointDomain d (n + m),
    ‖fderiv ℝ
        (fun y : NPointDomain d (n + m) =>
          bvt_F OS lgc (n + m)
            (xiShift ⟨n, Nat.lt_add_of_pos_right hm⟩ 0
              (fun k μ =>
                ↑(y k μ) +
                  ε * ↑(canonicalForwardConeDirection (d := d) (n + m) k μ) *
                    Complex.I)
              (t : ℂ))) y‖
      ≤ C_bd * (1 + ‖y‖) ^ N
```

  shell coefficient, and it sits strictly below full
  `Function.HasTemperateGrowth (fderiv ℝ shellCoeff)` because it only supplies
  the `n = 0` growth clause for that derivative-valued function;

  Before any theorem of the displayed shape can be stated and proved in

  Mathlib still requires the full theorem
  `Function.HasTemperateGrowth (fderiv ℝ shellCoeff)`, so after this first

  higher derivatives of `fderiv ℝ shellCoeff`;
- exact insertion-point verdict:
  the theorem belongs at the same live shell-coefficient seam in

  `bvt_F_canonicalXiShift_hasPolynomialGrowth` and before any first use of

```lean
    (fun y : NPointDomain d (n + m) =>
```

  that omission matters because the tail estimate needed for the generic
  `p`-net theorem is on `x - y`, so the proof must know both
  `x ∈ B` and `y ∈ B` in order to use
  `q (x - y) ≤ q x + q y < 2 M`;

- decomposition verdict:
  the generic nuclear-seminorm finite-net theorem **does** decompose one step
  smaller in theorem shape, but only to a local assembly theorem that keeps the
  head-net centres explicitly inside `B`;
- exact first internal proof obligation beneath it:
  choose `N` so that the tail of the nuclear series is uniformly small on
  differences `x - y` with `x, y ∈ B`,
  obtain a finite net for the head seminorm
  `∑ i : Fin N, ‖f i (x - y)‖ * c i`
  with centres still known to lie in `B`,
  and combine the two pieces using
  `Summable.sum_add_tsum_nat_add`
  plus the dominance inequality
  `p z ≤ ∑' n, ‖f n z‖ * c n`;
- whether that obligation is genuinely smaller or just the same blocker:
  genuinely smaller as a theorem shape,
  but not yet honestly available as a black-box consumer of the current live
  theorem surface, because the present finite-family theorem forgets the
  `y ∈ B` witness needed to bound the tail on `x - y`;
  without restoring that data, the candidate local theorem is just the same
  blocker restated;
- exact best Lean-ready theorem statement now required:
  still the full generic nuclear-seminorm finite-net theorem in
  `NuclearSpace.lean`;
  the smallest honest local statement below it would be:

  `∀ ε_head ε_tail > 0, ∀ N : ℕ,
      (∃ t : Finset V, (∀ y ∈ t, y ∈ B) ∧
        ∀ x ∈ B, ∃ y ∈ t, ∑ i : Fin N, ‖f i (x - y)‖ * c i < ε_head) ->
      (∀ x ∈ B, ∀ y ∈ B,
        ∑' n, ‖f (n + N) (x - y)‖ * c (n + N) < ε_tail) ->
      ∃ t : Finset V, ∀ x ∈ B, ∃ y ∈ t, p (x - y) < ε_head + ε_tail`;
- exact minimal hypotheses and codomain objects for that local theorem:
  fixed seminorm `p`,
  scalar-valued continuous linear maps `f n`,
  coefficients `c`,
  the dominance inequality
  `∀ z, p z ≤ ∑' n, ‖f n z‖ * c n`,
  one finite head-net with centres inside `B`,
  and one uniform tail estimate on differences of points in `B`;
  internally the only codomain objects remain a finite head-coordinate product
  `Fin N → ℝ` and the scalar tail series;

  immediately below
  `finite_weighted_coordinate_net_of_q_bounded`
  in
  `OSReconstruction/Wightman/NuclearSpaces/NuclearSpace.lean`,

  is the exact open-set endpoint:
  compactly supported Schwartz pairing equality on `U`, plus
  `ContinuousOn g U` and `ContinuousOn h U`, yields `Set.EqOn g h U`;
- `SCV.fourierLaplaceExtMultiDim_continuousWithinAt_realEmbed` at

  at

  already states the right shifted-side open-neighborhood continuity surface,

Exact smallest honest local-open theorem surface:

- the first useful theorem immediately above the raw pointwise blocker is not
  a new one-point continuity wrapper;
- it is a witness-local open-neighborhood boundary-agreement theorem for the
  fixed witness from
  `exists_flattened_bvt_F_dualCone_distribution_with_fourierLaplace_repr`:
  after fixing
  `q := n + m`,
  `j := ⟨n, Nat.lt_add_of_pos_right hm⟩`,
  `xflat0 := flattenCLEquivReal q (d + 1) (xiShift j 0 y t)`,
  and
  `G_t zflat :=
    bvt_F OS lgc q
      (xiShift j 0 ((flattenCLEquiv q (d + 1)).symm zflat) (t : ℂ))`,
  the smallest theorem that genuinely lets current SCV machinery fire is:
  `∃ Uflat, IsOpen Uflat ∧ xflat0 ∈ Uflat ∧ Set.EqOn g h Uflat`,
  where
  `g xflat := G_t (SCV.realEmbed xflat)` and
  `h xflat :=
    fourierLaplaceExtMultiDim
      (ForwardConeFlat d q)
      hCflat_open hCflat_conv hCflat_cone hCflat_salient
      Tflat (SCV.realEmbed xflat)`;
- evaluating that `EqOn` at `xflat0` yields the required
  `hrepr_boundary_shifted`.

What this theorem must assume to be provable from current proved SCV source:

- an open neighborhood `Uflat`;
- `ContinuousWithinAt G_t (SCV.TubeDomain (ForwardConeFlat d q))
    (SCV.realEmbed xflat)` for every `xflat ∈ Uflat`,
  which promotes to `ContinuousOn g Uflat`;
- `ContinuousWithinAt
    (fourierLaplaceExtMultiDim ... Tflat)
    (SCV.TubeDomain (ForwardConeFlat d q))
    (SCV.realEmbed xflat)` for every `xflat ∈ Uflat`,
  which promotes to `ContinuousOn h Uflat`;
- the local compact-support Schwartz pairing equality on `Uflat`, supplied by
  `fourierLaplace_boundary_recovery_on_open_of_tempered` on both sides plus the
  existing witness equality `hTflat_W`.

Verdict on whether current source already almost supplies those hypotheses:

- only as theorem shape on the shifted `bvt_F` side, not as trustworthy proved
  source;
- not even that on the exact FL side, where the needed boundary continuity
  theorem is still the live SCV `sorry`;
- so the first new mathematics is still the open-neighborhood boundary
  continuity/trace package itself, not a downstream positivity transport
  theorem below theorem-(1).

Exact proposed theorem 1:

- primitive near-boundary estimate only, with no wrapper drift:
  `fourierLaplaceExtMultiDim_uniform_bound_near_boundary`
  stating
  `∀ (η : Fin m → ℝ), η ∈ C →
      ∃ (C_bd : ℝ) (N : ℕ) (δ : ℝ), C_bd > 0 ∧ δ > 0 ∧
        ∀ (x : Fin m → ℝ) (ε : ℝ), 0 < ε → ε < δ →
          ‖fourierLaplaceExtMultiDim C hC_open hC_conv hC_cone hC_salient T
              (fun i => ↑(x i) + ↑ε * ↑(η i) * I)‖ ≤
            C_bd * (1 + ‖x‖) ^ N`;
- why theorem 1 is first:
  this is exactly the missing `uniform_bound` field already required by both
  `HasFourierLaplaceReprTempered` and `HasFourierLaplaceReprRegular`, and the
  current primitive source does not supply it because the proved growth theorem
  still allows the `ε⁻ᴹ` blowup.

Exact proposed theorem 2:

- boundary-valued regular representative theorem, not a claim about the zero
  extension itself:
  `fourierLaplaceExtMultiDim_exists_regular_boundary_repr`
  stating
  `∃ G : (Fin m → ℂ) → ℂ,
      (∀ z, z ∈ SCV.TubeDomain C →
        G z =
          fourierLaplaceExtMultiDim C hC_open hC_conv hC_cone hC_salient T z) ∧
      HasFourierLaplaceReprRegular C G`;
- why theorem 2 is the next step:
  the live seam needs the boundary-continuity fields on a function whose real
  trace is the intended boundary value; the primitive definition
  `fourierLaplaceExtMultiDim` is the zero extension off the tube, so those
  fields cannot honestly be attached to that zero extension without a new
  boundary-valued representative.

- the first honest mathematical target is theorem 1 above;
- theorem 2 is the first honest continuation after theorem 1;
- no Lean theorem statement was added in this pass, because no genuinely tiny
  compile-clean statement was forced by current source.

  [OSToWightmanPositivity.lean:4224](/home/claw/.openclaw/workspace/OSreconstruction/OSReconstruction/Wightman/Reconstruction/WickRotation/OSToWightmanPositivity.lean:4224)
  still gives only convergence of test integrals to a functional `T_t`, not pointwise limits to a function on a complement-side open set.
- Therefore the supplier theorem itself is still not source-ready:
  no checked source object yet provides the required `B_t`, `ContinuousOn B_t Uflat`, and pointwise `htraceUflat` package for the flattened fixed-time continuation.
